t_ch: 咳，打印机的事过去以后，又过了一段时间，我在商业区的一个巷子里又撞见了那个蓝毛。
t_en: Ahem, sometime later, after the counterfeiting scam, I bumped into that same blue-haired guy again in an alley in the Commercial District.

t_ch: 我本来应该直接叫银鬃铁卫去抓人的。但是我当时怒上心头，非要去跟他讨个说法……
t_en: I should have had the Silvermane Guards arrest him, but I was so angry and I needed an explanation...

t_ch: 没想到他反而一脸严肃地问我「你是不是一直盯着打印机看」——
t_en: Instead, he asked me with a straight face, "Were you staring at the printer?"

t_ch: 他斥责我根本没看说明书，还说我不识货，暴殄天物。他甚至还提出要全款回收「平行世界打印机」，找别的买家……
t_en: He accused me of not reading the manual and wasting such a genius invention. He even offered to refund the full payment so that he could find another buyer for the parallel universe printer.

t_ch: 他还说，「如果操作者一直死盯着打印机看，平行世界传输现象就不会发生——要想不干涉传输过程，只有借助『以太透镜』进行观测。」
t_en: He also said, "If the operator keeps staring at the printer, the parallel universe transmission can't happen. The only way to avoid interfering with the transmission process is to observe it with the help of an aether lens."

t_ch: …所以你又去买了透镜？
t_en: ...So you bought an aether lens?

t_ch: …这不是自然而然的事吗？那骗子一本正经地跟我理论，又把我给说服了……
t_en: Doesn't that sound reasonable? So I was tricked again by his apparent sincerity...

t_ch: 我现在算是明白了，听好了——打着「平行世界」的旗号推广商品的家伙，十有八九都是骗子！
t_en: Now I know that nine out of ten people who try to associate their products with any kind of parallel universe theory are scammers!

t_ch: 「平行世界」真实存在。
t_en: Parallel universes actually exist.

t_ch: 哈哈，逗我玩是吧？你指望我相信你吗？
t_en: Haha, are you kidding me? Do you really expect me to believe that?

t_ch: 还是说…难道你是那蓝头发的同伙？
t_en: Or... are you working with the blue-haired guy?

t_ch: 走开，走开！你再不走，我就让银鬃铁卫来逮捕你了！
t_en: Get out! If you don't leave, I'll have Silvermane Guards come and arrest you!

t_ch: 那你是真的厉害。
t_en: Wow, I am soo scared.

t_ch: 尽管嘲笑我吧，但你一定要记住我的教训…总之，警惕「平行世界」！
t_en: Laugh at me if you want, just remember what I told you... Be careful with anything related to parallel universes!

t_ch: 没事了。
t_en: That's all.

t_ch: 我会小心的。
t_en: I'll be careful.

t_ch: 这就对了！千万别上了他的当！
t_en: That's right! Don't fall for his tricks!

t_ch: 我好像记得你，年轻人。怎么，读过防骗教程后有问题想问了吗？
t_en: I remember you, young person. Do you have any questions after reading the fraud prevention tutorial?

t_ch: 还是说，你也想加入「深蓝骗局受害者互助会」？
t_en: Or do you want to join the Dark-Blue Scam Victims Association?

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 最近啊，我正以互助会的名义准备诉愿信，打算找布洛妮娅大人一趟。
t_en: Recently... I'm preparing an appeal on behalf of the Association. I plan on meeting with Lady Bronya.

t_ch: 为什么是互助会？
t_en: Why the Association?

t_ch: 那当然是因为这件事关系到我们互助会的根本利益了。
t_en: Obviously, this issue involves the Association's fundamental interests.

t_ch: 为什么是布洛妮娅？
t_en: Why Bronya?

t_ch: 那当然是因为只有布洛妮娅大人能解决我们的问题了。
t_en: Because Lady Bronya clearly is the only person who can solve our issue.

t_ch: 别激动，有什么诉求坐下来谈…
t_en: Take it easy. If there's an issue, we can sit down and discuss it...

t_ch: 啊？你说什么呢？
t_en: Huh? What are you talking about?

t_ch: …嗐，你误会了！我们只是想让布洛妮娅大人关注一下「深蓝骗局」——这可关系到我们贝洛伯格的长治久安啊。
t_en: ...You misunderstood what I meant! All we want is to make Lady Bronya aware of the Dark-Blue Scam. This issue has implications for the peace and stability of Belobog.

t_ch: 听说布洛妮娅大人要比前任大守护者开明得多。她现在正在克里珀堡广开言路，人人有份…那我们「深蓝骗局受害者互助会」自然不能放过这个机会。
t_en: I heard that the Supreme Guardian Lady Bronya is far more open-minded than the former Supreme Guardian. She is encouraging people at Qlipoth Fort to air their opinions. Everyone has a responsibility to speak... Well, the Dark-Blue Scam Victims Association naturally isn't going to let this opportunity get away.

t_ch: 我们的诉求也很简单：银鬃铁卫内部应当立即成立一支「深蓝骗局专案大队」，在贝洛伯格城内展开地毯式搜索，围剿这个蓝毛诈骗犯……
t_en: Our request is quite simple. The Silvermane Guards should immediately establish a Dark-Blue Scam Special Task Force that will launch a city-wide search in Belobog to round up this blue-haired con artist...

t_ch: 抓到之后呢？
t_en: What will happen after the task force catches him?

t_ch: 这还不简单？把这个混蛋五花大绑，关在克里珀堡的地下室里严刑拷问，直到他把犯下的所有罪行供出来为止；接着再把他吊在城墙外面……
t_en: That's easy. They should tie this wretched villain up and lock him beneath Qlipoth Fort to be questioned under torture. The interrogation would continue until he has confessed all of his crimes. After that, he'll be hanged outside the city...

t_ch: …很有野心。
t_en: ...That's ambitious.

t_ch: 你是在夸我吗？我就当是吧。
t_en: Are you praising me? I'll assume you are.

t_ch: 总之，我们一定要找到那个蓝毛骗子，刻不容缓！
t_en: The point is, we simply must find that blue-haired swindler. We cannot tolerate such criminal activity!

t_ch: 突然感觉桑博怪可怜的…
t_en: I suddenly feel pity for Sampo.

t_ch: 桑博？哦，你说的是那个诈骗犯的假名吧？
t_en: Sampo? That con artist's alias?

t_ch: 你可千万不能同情他啊！不然轻则贪小失大，重则倾家荡产……
t_en: Don't ever sympathize with him! You'll either end up losing some money at the minimum, or even see your family fortune go down the drain...

t_ch: …很有野心。
t_en: ...That's ambitious.

t_ch: 你是在夸我吗？我就当是吧。
t_en: Are you praising me? I'll assume you are.

t_ch: 总之，我们一定要找到那个蓝毛骗子，刻不容缓！
t_en: The point is, we simply must find that blue-haired swindler. We cannot tolerate such criminal activity!

t_ch: 突然感觉桑博怪可怜的…
t_en: I suddenly feel pity for Sampo.

t_ch: 桑博？哦，你说的是那个诈骗犯的假名吧？
t_en: Sampo? That con artist's alias?

t_ch: 你可千万不能同情他啊！不然轻则贪小失大，重则倾家荡产……
t_en: Don't ever sympathize with him! You'll either end up losing some money at the minimum, or even see your family fortune go down the drain...

t_ch: ——打住。民事纠纷请找银鬃铁卫报案，不要企图绕过检方直接与法官议论……
t_en: ...Wait! Please file a report with the Silvermane Guards if you encounter any civil disputes. Please don't try to bypass the prosecution and discuss it with the judge directly.

t_ch: 法官？
t_en: Judge?

t_ch: 我知道你想问什么，既然自称法官，为什么会站在大街上，而不是待在法院里——听过「流动法官」这个词吗？
t_en: I know what you want to ask. As a judge, how is it that I am standing out in the street instead of in the courthouse, right? Have you heard of the term "Roaming Judge"?

t_ch: …唉，算了。听着挺高大上，实际上就是个协助铁卫工作的外派法律顾问而已。跟法院里的工作相比，这活计简直就是浪费时间……
t_en: ...*sigh* Forget about it. It sounds impressive, but I'm just a legal advisor sent to help the guards. Compared to my work at the court, this is a major waste of time...

t_ch: 跟法院里的工作相比，这活计简直就是浪费时间……
t_en: Compared to working in the court, this is a waste of time...

t_ch: 浪费？
t_en: Waste of time?

t_ch: 也不能这么说吧…
t_en: Well, you shouldn't put it like that...

t_ch: 嘁，我现在的工作就是和每天来找茬的老头老太太理论，这还不算浪费时间吗？我可是贝洛伯格法学院的高材生，凭什么每天只能处理些家长里短的琐事？
t_en: Don't you think it's a waste of my time to have me out here trying to talk sense into the elderly every day? I was a straight-A student at Belobog Law School. Why am I dealing with trivial family stuff?

t_ch: 凭我的实力，就应该去负责更复杂、更有价值的大案！那种案件才能体现我的价值！你懂我在说什么吧？
t_en: I should be in charge of more complex, important cases! That's where I shine! You know what I'm talking about, right?

t_ch: …你那是什么表情？算了，我看你和那群老头老太太也差不了多少…要是你碰上了什么悬而难决的大案，记得来找我。
t_en: ...What's that look on your face? Forget it. You aren't much different from those old-timers... Just come to me when you have a big case.

t_ch: 我想和你聊聊…
t_en: Can we talk...?

t_ch: 感觉你很烦躁啊。
t_en: You seem upset.

t_ch: 哈？有那么明显吗，这都被你看出来了？
t_en: Huh? Is it that obvious?

t_ch: 成天和一帮什么都不懂的老头老太太吵架，换做是你，你能不烦么？
t_en: Dealing with a bunch of ignorant old people, every day — you would be annoyed too if you were me.

t_ch: 你都帮他们处理什么法律问题？
t_en: What kind of legal issues do you help them with?

t_ch: 「法律问题」？你可太看得起贝洛伯格的老百姓了。他们来找我解决的问题和法律可一点都不沾边，无非就是些家长里短的抱怨。
t_en: Legal issues? You think too highly of the people of Belobog. What they come to me for has nothing to do with the law. It's all complaints about daily life.

t_ch: 我随口就能给你来一段：反正就是他本人隐忍退让、委曲求全、顾全大局；另一方不知好歹、得寸进尺、真不是人。
t_en: It's always the same thing. The complainant tells me they have been altruistic and taken the big picture into consideration and the offending party has taken advantage of them and is being ungrateful.

t_ch: 你在煦日节的家族聚会上能听到超过一万种类似街坊故事的变体，那些故事说来说去，不就是换着花样的「道德绑架」吗？我反正是一点兴趣都没有。
t_en: You can hear thousands of stories like this at Solwarm Festival family gatherings. Those stories are, in the end, moral blackmail, which I'm not interested in at all.

t_ch: 但我毕竟是个法律工作者，给民众普法是我的职责——每到这个环节，那群人就开始假装听不明白了——
t_en: But I am a legal practitioner after all, so it is my duty to help the public get to know the law. Every time it gets to this part, they start to pretend like they don't understand me.

t_ch: 谁叫法律就是公正无私，不偏袒任何人呢？
t_en: But who's to blame? After all, the law is always fair and impartial, showing favor to no one?

t_ch: 真不容易啊。
t_en: Wow. You have a hard job.

t_ch: 听着就头皮发麻。
t_en: Just hearing about it makes my head spin.

t_ch: 那可不！什么破流动法官，我真是一秒都做不下去了，唉。
t_en: I know, right!? I can't go on being a Roaming Judge for a second longer.

t_ch: 什么案子才算大？
t_en: What kind of cases do you consider important?

t_ch: 纠察贪污腐败，惩治恶党奸徒…再不济也得涉火涉骗吧？
t_en: Corruption-related, with evil organizations or personnel involved. Or at least arms smuggling... or scams...

t_ch: 算了，你就当是听我说梦话吧。现在贝洛伯格治安可好得很，就算真撞上了大案也轮不着我来审……
t_en: Forget it. Just pretend like I'm talking nonsense. Belobog is too safe to have cases like that. Even if there is a big case, it's out of my reach...

t_ch: 没事了。
t_en: That's all.

t_ch: 行吧，再见。
t_en: All right, bye.

t_ch: 呃…你好像来找过我。怎么，碰到什么大案了吗？
t_en: Hmm... I remember you. Got some big case?

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 最近可是有个大案子啊！——可惜还是轮不到我来审……
t_en: I've got a big case on my hands! It's a shame I'll never be the one to try the case...

t_ch: 不对，应该说这案子根本无从审起…就是布洛妮娅大人本人来了也不行。
t_en: I guess more specifically, I should say that there's no way this case can be tried... Even Lady Bronya herself would not be able to.

t_ch: 什么大案子？
t_en: What's this case you're talking about?

t_ch: 布洛妮娅都不行？
t_en: Even Bronya can't handle it?

t_ch: 是啊，前任大守护者可可利亚…在之前贝洛伯格北面的裂界骚动中牺牲了。她的女儿布洛妮娅小姐接替了她的位置。
t_en: Yeah. Former Supreme Guardian Cocolia... passed during the Fragmentum disturbance that happened north of Belobog. Her daughter Bronya took over her position after that.

t_ch: 布洛妮娅大人声称可可利亚「前往了裂界的根源，以生命为代价守护了贝洛伯格」…但这是她一家之言，谁知道究竟发生了什么呢？反正也没有别的证人……
t_en: Lady Bronya stated that Cocolia went to the source of the Fragmentum and sacrificed herself to protect Belobog... But that's just what she is saying. Who knows what actually happened? There were no other witnesses after all...

t_ch: …咳，不过这一切都与我无关。变化就是机会，我要做的事情只有抓住这个机会，想办法让自己坐在法庭上…而不是接着和这帮老头老太太吵架。
t_en: ...Hmph. Then again, that all has nothing to do with me. Change is opportunity. All I have to do now is take hold of this opportunity and find a way to get myself seated at the court... I don't need to keep arguing with those old hags.

t_ch: 祝你成功。
t_en: Here's to your success.

t_ch: 借你吉言…虽然我觉得可能性不大就是了。也许这亚历珊德拉广场就是我的归宿吧……
t_en: Hopefully your words will bring me good luck... But I think my chances are not particularly good. Maybe Alexandra Plaza is just where I belong...

t_ch: 我觉得没戏。
t_en: You don't stand a chance.

t_ch: 你说得对，我也觉得没戏。也许这亚历珊德拉广场就是我的归宿吧……
t_en: You're right. There's no way it'll work out. Maybe Alexandra Plaza is just where I belong...

t_ch: 考虑过当大守护者的法律顾问吗？
t_en: Have you thought about being a legal adviser to the Supreme Guardian?

t_ch: …你疯啦？！就凭我？不行不行，法律可是神圣的，我这种小法官可配不上这职位……
t_en: ...Are you out of your mind!? Me? No way. The law is sacred. A small judge like me isn't worthy of a role like that...

t_ch: 去去去，你赶紧走吧，可别让那边的铁卫听到我们在议论这些……
t_en: Scram. Don't let the guards hear us talking about this...

t_ch: …嗯？以前好像没见过你。是第一次来买书吗？
t_en: Hmm... I don't think I've met you before. Is this your first time visiting the bookstore?

t_ch: 都有些什么书？
t_en: What books do you have?

t_ch: 我先看会书，你选好了就告诉我吧。
t_en: I'll be here reading. Let me know when you've made your selections.

t_ch: 我想和你聊聊…
t_en: Can we talk...?

t_ch: 好呀。说吧，你想聊什么？
t_en: Alright. Let's hear it. What do you want to chat about?

t_ch: 生意怎么样？
t_en: How's business?

t_ch: 还不错啊，贝洛伯格爱读书的人可不少呢。
t_en: Not bad. Belobog has a lot of people who love to read.

t_ch: 不过，要是有些人看完书能付钱就更好了……
t_en: But it would be nice if everyone paid for the books they read...

t_ch: 有什么好书推荐吗？
t_en: Do you have any recommendations?

t_ch: 哼哼，这你可问对人了。你喜欢什么类型的书？
t_en: You came to the right person. What kind of books do you like?

t_ch: 我想读奇幻小说。
t_en: I'd like to read some fantasy.

t_ch: 奇幻小说呀…我会推荐这本哦。这本书讲述的是从世界之外漂流而来的旅行者为寻找血亲踏上旅途的故事，在年轻人当中非常受欢迎…我也很喜欢！
t_en: Fantasy...? You'll love this one. It's about a traveler from a different world who is on a journey to find their family. It's very popular among young people... I loved it!

t_ch: 我想读科幻小说。
t_en: I like reading science fiction.

t_ch: 科幻小说…你会喜欢主角一行搭乘蒸汽火车在银河中穿行、探索星际文明的故事吗？这本小说在科幻爱好者群体中广受好评哦！
t_en: Science fiction...? Would you like to try reading a story about a group traveling by steam train through the cosmos to explore interstellar civilizations? This book is widely acclaimed!

t_ch: 我想读推理小说。
t_en: I love mystery novels.

t_ch: 原来你也是推理小说爱好者…这本怎么样？讲的是少女安乐椅侦探和搭档一起解决各种离奇案件、发掘隐秘真相的故事——虽然不算热门，但是质量相当高的作品呢。
t_en: I'm also a fan of mystery novels... What do you think about this one? It's about a young girl, Detective Anraku, and her associates, solving strange cases and uncovering hidden truths. It isn't exactly a hit but it's quality writing.

t_ch: 你先忙吧。
t_en: I'll leave you to your work.

t_ch: 不打扰你啦。
t_en: I should stop bothering you now.

t_ch: …啊！不好意思，读书太入迷了没注意到你。想看看书吗？
t_en: ...Ah! Sorry, I was so caught up in my reading that I didn't notice you. Do you need help with some books?

t_ch: 让我看看…
t_en: Let me see...

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 最近吗？最近我读了好多书，手里的存货又要读完了…改天是不是得再去进货了呢？
t_en: Lately? I've been doing a lot of reading. I'm almost done reading all the books I have in stock... Maybe I'll need to get some new books in?

t_ch: 原来进货是为了给自己读啊…
t_en: So, you've been getting new stock to give yourself things to read...

t_ch: 嘿嘿，只是先向出版社要样书啦。我对书籍内容的要求可是很严格的。连我这关都过不了的书，可别想流到市场上。
t_en: Hehe. I just ask publishers for advanced copies of books. I have high standards when it comes to content, so if a book can't satisfy me, then they shouldn't even think about putting it on the market.

t_ch: 如果样书都没问题的话，我才会向出版社要大货，到那时候再正式对外发售——这就是我的售书方法论！
t_en: If an advanced copy is okay, I'll tell the publisher I want to place an order for that book. Then I'll officially put it on sale for the general public. That's how I sell books!

t_ch: 你这是利用职务之便吧…
t_en: You're just taking advantage of your position...

t_ch: 唔…你要这么说，我也没办法反驳你。
t_en: Oh... I mean, when you put it that way, I can't say that you're wrong.

t_ch: 不过有些书的内容真的称得上是粗制滥造…我怀疑是出版社内部有编辑在偷懒。要我说，我这可是在帮读者一个大忙！
t_en: There's some content out there that tells me a publisher is churning out books without any regard to quality... I suspect that the publisher has a lazy editor. From my perspective, I'm doing the reader a solid!

t_ch: 那有打听到什么其他消息吗？
t_en: Have you heard any other news?

t_ch: 有打听到什么其他消息吗？
t_en: Have you heard any other news?

t_ch: 啊，说到这个…你听说了吗？据说那本《雪国冒险奇谭》可能有机会重新公开发售了哦！
t_en: Ah. Well... have you heard? Supposedly, there's a chance that the Tale of the Winterlands can be openly sold again!

t_ch: 那可真是一本好书呀，之前被查禁了真的很可惜…你也是《奇谭》的书迷吗？我现在正在全程跟进相关信息哦，如果有什么新进展肯定第一时间告诉你！
t_en: That book series is simply fantastic. It was such a shame that it was banned before... Are you a Tales fan? I've been keeping up with all the news about it. If I hear any updates, I'll be sure to tell you right away!

t_ch: 报纸！今天的报纸！快来看看咯！
t_en: We got the news! Today's paper! Hot off the press!

t_ch: 哟，竟然来了个生面孔！要买份报吗？
t_en: Hah! A new face! Interested in a newspaper?

t_ch: 给我来份报纸。
t_en: Yes, please.

t_ch: 来聊聊天吧…
t_en: Let's have a chat...

t_ch: 哎哟，这可头疼了，现在可是工作时间哪？不过您要是愿意买份报的话……
t_en: Sorry, you caught me during work hours, but if you want to buy a newspaper...

t_ch: 生意怎么样？
t_en: How's business?

t_ch: 生意？嘿嘿，至少填饱肚子是不用愁咯。
t_en: Business? Hehe. Just enough to keep from starving.

t_ch: 不过嘛，走街串巷沿街叫卖到底是个体力活…但您要是能给我贡献点销售额，我兴许就能讨些不一样的食，那可是再好不过！
t_en: Selling these in the streets is hard work... It would be nice if you could contribute a little to my profits. Then, I may be able to afford to eat something different for once!

t_ch: 怎么说？您有新闻看，我能吃顿好——各取所需！如何，这笔买卖不亏吧？
t_en: What do you think? You get the news, I get a good meal. It's a win-win! A good deal, right?

t_ch: 最近有什么大新闻？
t_en: Any big news lately?

t_ch: 哟，这您可是提了个好问题！等我来给您翻翻……
t_en: Hmm, that's a good question! Let me see...

t_ch: 这个怎么样？——「黑衣人来到波瓦大臣身旁——只说了一句话，大臣便神色紧张地离开了宴会。到底发生了什么？」
t_en: How about this one — What did the man in black tell Mr. Bova, the minister, that made him leave the party in a hurry?

t_ch: 发生了什么？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，您甭着急！再看看这个：「七人聚众火锅，六人相继昏倒；调查发现火锅里并无有害物质，凶手如何达成完美犯罪？」
t_en: Hold on! Check out this one — Six out of seven people fainted while eating hot pot. No harmful substances were found in the hot pot. How did this perfect crime happen?

t_ch: 怎么做到的？
t_en: How?

t_ch: 您想知道？
t_en: You want to know?

t_ch: 想啊！
t_en: Of course!

t_ch: 那我可是有个妙招，能解您燃眉之急！
t_en: I have a great idea to help with your urgent needs!

t_ch: 来，您看看这份报纸——谜底就在这上边印着啦！只要您肯舍我点冬城盾……
t_en: Here, the answer is right in this newspaper! It's yours as long as you give me some shield...

t_ch: 你想急死我？
t_en: Are you messing with me?

t_ch: 嗐，话怎么能这么说呢！
t_en: Huh? No!

t_ch: 我这就把答案告诉您——只要您肯舍我点冬城盾就行！
t_en: I'll tell you the answer, right away, after you give me a few shield!

t_ch: 你想气死我？
t_en: Are you for real?

t_ch: 您消消气，消消气！嗐，这不都是为了生意嘛，还请您理解……
t_en: Take it easy! Take it easy! It's just how the business works. I'm sure you understand...

t_ch: 你先忙吧。
t_en: I'll leave you to your work.

t_ch: 你接着忙，先不打扰了。
t_en: I'll leave you to your work.

t_ch: 号外！号外！先睹为快！
t_en: News! We got the news! Don't miss it!

t_ch: 让我看看…
t_en: Let me see...

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 最近？最近那可是过得红红火火，哈哈…您也知道吧？这贝洛伯格的情况简直是要翻天覆地啦！
t_en: Recently? I've been living large. Haha... You've heard, right? Belobog is about to go through some big changes!

t_ch: 我当然知道。
t_en: Of course, I've heard.

t_ch: 哈哈，您的消息也真是灵通！
t_en: Haha, you're really up with the play!

t_ch: 悄悄告诉您，我从报社的人那儿听说，前任大守护者出事的时候，在场的只有布洛妮娅大人一个人…怎么样，可疑吧？
t_en: On the down-low... I heard from my contacts at the newspapers that when the old Supreme Guardian had her accident, the only one at the scene was Lady Bronya... doesn't that seem suspicious?

t_ch: 哇，真的假的？
t_en: Wow, are you serious?

t_ch: 那还有假？您看看，我可是卖报的，每天有什么新闻我都是第一个知道——都是一手消息，千真万确！
t_en: Would I lie to you? Look, I sell newspapers for a living, and I'm the first to hear the news every day — all first-hand, every word of it is true!

t_ch: 悄悄告诉您，我从报社的人那儿听说，前任大守护者出事的时候，在场的只有布洛妮娅大人一个人…怎么样，可疑吧？
t_en: On the down-low... I heard from my contacts at the newspapers that when the old Supreme Guardian had her accident, the only one at the scene was Lady Bronya... doesn't that seem suspicious?

t_ch: 实不相瞒，我亲眼看着大守护者…
t_en: To tell you the truth, I saw with my own eyes that the Supreme Guardian...

t_ch: 嚯，那您可得好好说说…那大守护者到底出什么事了？
t_en: Huh, then you have to tell me about it... What happened to the Supreme Guardian?

t_ch: 哈哈，开个玩笑！您就甭拿我打趣了。瞧瞧告诉您，我跟报社的人确认过了，前任大守护者出事的时候，在场的只有布洛妮娅大人一个人……
t_en: Haha, just kidding! Don't make fun of me. On the down-low... I had it confirmed from my contacts at the newspapers that when the old Supreme Guardian had her accident, the only one at the scene was Lady Bronya...

t_ch: …咳咳，不论如何，现在的贝洛伯格可在风口浪尖上——我们这行可得好好抓住这个机会哪！所谓「机不可失，失不再来」…您说是吧？
t_en: ...Ahem, well no matter what, Belobog is in the teeth of a storm now — and in this line of business, you've got to take ya chances! You know what they say: "An opportunity lost is an opportunity wasted"... right?

t_ch: 干后勤，干后勤，每天就是干后勤，唉……
t_en: Logistics, logistics, all I do is logistics every day, *sigh*...

t_ch: 十来岁的姑娘当领导，五十岁的老头儿干后勤……
t_en: Get a teenage girl to be the boss, get a <unbreak>50</unbreak>-year-old geezer to do logistics...

t_ch: 二十多的小伙当大官，五十岁的老头儿干后勤……
t_en: Get a <unbreak>20</unbreak>-year-old boy to be an official, get a <unbreak>50</unbreak>-year-old geezer to do logistics...
*sigh*

t_ch: 哎，这些「地火」的人…还有那些流浪者，没完没了的好烦啊…就不能安分点吗。
t_en: Ack, these Wildfire guys... and the vagrants, they're so annoying... can't you just chill out?

t_ch: 你不喜欢「地火」？
t_en: You don't like Wildfire?

t_ch: 我可没有讨厌「地火」，我只是觉得他们太过理想化了。别看我是个家庭主妇，但外面的情况我还是知道的。
t_en: I don't hate Wildfire. I just think they're too idealistic. I might be just a housewife, but I know a thing or two about the outside world.

t_ch: 吃喝拉撒、装备后勤，地底样样都缺。没有这些物资保障，你拿什么保卫自己的家园？那不就是空想嘛！
t_en: Food, water, waste disposal, logistics — it's a mess from head to toe in the Underworld. Without these basics guaranteed, how do you plan on defending your home? Why, it's just a daydream!

t_ch: 你很讨厌流浪者？
t_en: You seem to hate the vagrants.

t_ch: 那不然呢？成天就知道惹事，安分点找个稳定的工作不好吗？
t_en: Of course. They stir up trouble all day. They should settle down and get stable jobs.

t_ch: 是是是，我知道那些怪物害得有些人没了家园，但有手有脚的找份工作很难吗？凡事总不能让环境迁就你，你得去适应环境，懂吧？
t_en: I know some people lost their homes because of the monsters, but is it that hard for able-bodied people to find jobs? You can't let your situation dictate your predicament, instead you have to adapt. Do you get me?

t_ch: 所以啊，我是这么教育我家小孩的——「你不听爸爸妈妈的话，长大以后就只能去当流浪者咯！」
t_en: So that's how I taught my kids — "If you don't listen to your parents, you'll end up as a vagrant when you grow up!"

t_ch: 你问这么多干吗？看你也不像流浪者，难道你是「地火」的人？不对，看你这身衣服不像是下层的风格…莫非你是从上层区来的？
t_en: Why are you asking so many questions? You don't look like a vagrant. Are you part of the Wildfire? No, you don't dress like you're from the Underworld... Are you from the Overworld?

t_ch: 你误会了。
t_en: You misunderstand.

t_ch: 我只是个路过的。
t_en: I'm just passing through.

t_ch: …啧，我还以为是个从上面来的大人物…你来找我干吗，有什么事吗？
t_en: ...Gee, I thought you were some big shot from above... So what's your deal?

t_ch: 我有点好奇…
t_en: I'm surprised that...

t_ch: 你知道上面的事？
t_en: You know about up there?

t_ch: 那当然，以前我和孩子他爸刚刚认识的时候，还经常在上面的餐厅里烛光晚餐呢，我又不是镇子上这些年轻人，我怎么可能……
t_en: Of course. Back when my husband and I first met, we used to have romantic candlelit dinners up there in the restaurants. I'm not just some country kid, tsk...

t_ch: 呃，等等，你是问我上面的现况吗？那我还真不知道，要不你给我讲讲？
t_en: Uh, hang on — do you mean up above right now? I really don't know. Could you tell me about it?

t_ch: 你如何评价铁卫撤离一事？
t_en: What's your view of the evacuation of the Guards?

t_ch: 有什么好评价的呀，我听说这是可可利亚大人的命令，但我想总归有她的道理吧…她可是大守护者诶，我不相信她还能相信谁?
t_en: What's there to say? I heard it's on Madam Cocolia's orders, but she has to have her reasons... She's the Supreme Guardian after all. If I can't trust her, who can I trust?

t_ch: 所以…我觉得吧，铁卫们迟早会重回下层保卫我们的！大守护者一定不会抛弃我们。
t_en: So... I think that sooner or later the Guards will return to the Underworld to protect us! The Supreme Guardian will never abandon us!

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 没什么事，我先走了。
t_en: Nothing. Sorry, I have to go.

t_ch: 我跟你说，你别学那群流浪者一样，总以为自己比可可利亚大人他们聪明。
t_en: I'm telling you, don't you dare be like those vagrants and dream that you're smarter than Madam Cocolia and the government.

t_ch: 大守护者不会抛弃我们的，而银鬃铁卫也迟早会回来的，不信的话咱们走着瞧！
t_en: The Supreme Guardian will never abandon us. And the Silvermane Guards have to return some time. Just you wait and see!

t_ch: 孩子他爸最近回来得早，最近难得有几天清净日子，干脆加个菜好了。
t_en: My husband has been coming back early these days, and peace and quiet has been in short supply. Let's add another dish.

t_ch: 不过…又得自己买菜自己煮…哎，真怀念能以前在上面的餐厅约会的日子…也不知道那个铁卫大哥现在过得怎么样……
t_en: But... I'll have to buy everything and cook it myself... Ack, I really miss those days when we could date up there... I wonder what that man from the Guards is up to these days...

t_ch: 诶？是你啊，吓我一跳，我以为是哪个不长眼的流浪者呢。
t_en: Ah? It's you. You startled me. I thought it was some dense vagrants.

t_ch: 怎么，你找我有什么事吗？
t_en: What do you want from me?

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 和以前一样呗，不过…你听说了吗？上下层区马上就要开放了！我就知道大守护者不会放弃我们，之前撤走铁卫果然是形势所迫吧…太好了，我们有希望了！
t_en: Same as before. But... have you heard? The Underworld and Overworld are being connected soon! I knew the Supreme Guardian would never abandon us, and withdrawing Guards was just out of circumstances... Great! We have hope!

t_ch: 你觉得是大守护者的功劳？
t_en: Do you think it's the work of the Supreme Guardian?

t_ch: 那当然！大守护者高瞻远瞩，人家哪样不比我们普通人强。可惜啊，之前镇子上还有些人一直在诋毁大守护者——
t_en: Of course, it is! The Supreme Guardian is far-sighted, and more powerful than us commoners in every way. It's a pity then that some villagers have taken to slandering the name of the Supreme Guardian...

t_ch: 尤其是「地火」和流浪者那帮人，之前成天就知道抱怨大守护者抛弃了下层区…这下把她盼回来了吧？哼，回头我倒要看看那帮人怎么解释！
t_en: Especially that Wildfire and the vagrants, endlessly complaining about how the Supreme Guardian abandoned the Underworld... Now she's back. Hmph, I'll have to see how they spin this one.

t_ch: 你好像很激动。
t_en: You seem excited.

t_ch: 你说这话是什么意思？难道你就不觉得激动吗？
t_en: What do you mean? Don't you feel excited, too?

t_ch: 等银鬃铁卫正式回归下层区，这地方的未来不是肉眼可见的光明吗？
t_en: Once the Silvermane Guards return to the Underworld, the glorious future of this place will be obvious to all.

t_ch: 哎…银鬃铁卫的各位怎么还不下来呀？有他们在镇子上巡逻，我这心里才有安全感啊！
t_en: Hey... Why haven't the Silvermane Guards come down yet? It only feels safe to have them patrol around the town!

t_ch: 没看见我在忙吗？有话快说，没事别烦我……
t_en: Can't you see I'm busy? Spit it out and stop bothering me...

t_ch: 是你啊，又见面了！上次走得匆忙，还没来得及向你道谢呢。
t_en: ...Ah, it's you again! The last time we met, I left in a hurry. I never got a chance to thank you.

t_ch: 再自我介绍一下，我是大矿区的承包商、矿队工头安东尼娜。如你之前在矿区所见，这地底有不少人跟着我混口饭吃。
t_en: I'd like to introduce myself again. I'm Antonia, the developer responsible for the Great Mine and the supervisor for the mining teams. As you saw in the mine, a lot of people in the underground count on me to make a living.

t_ch: 矿头不用去矿场监工吗？
t_en: Don't you need to supervise the miners?

t_ch: 监工的事，交给采掘现场的小队长就行了。
t_en: The team leaders are responsible for supervision.

t_ch: 我的工作就是统筹人力，安排工期。亲自下场不仅帮不上忙，还会碍了矿工们的事。何必给大家找不自在呢？
t_en: My job is to coordinate personnel and arrange work schedules. My presence down in the mines will only hinder the miners' work. Why make everyone uncomfortable?

t_ch: 矿头一定很有钱吧？
t_en: Supervisors make a lot of money, right?

t_ch: 你的问题还真尖锐…咳，这么说吧，下层区的大家过得都很拮据，相对于大多数人来说…我手头还算富余。
t_en: You are quite direct... Ahem, let's put it this way, everyone in the Underworld is practically living on scraps... so compared to most of them, I have a decent life.

t_ch: 这地底算得上值钱的玩意就只有地髓了——碰巧，我手里最不缺的就是地髓。
t_en: The only thing that's worth anything in Underground is Geomarrow, which I happen to have a ton of on hand.

t_ch: 对了，听说你们在帮「地火」办事？我提醒你们一句，奥列格那帮人虽然很讲义气，但是兜里其实根本没几个子儿。
t_en: By the way, I heard that you were working for Wildfire. A kind reminder for you, Oleg's crew takes care of their own, but their pockets aren't deep.

t_ch: 要是各位在资金周转方面遇到了什么困难，欢迎随时来找我帮忙。
t_en: Feel free to come to me when you have financial problems.

t_ch: 怎么样，还有别的什么事吗？没有的话，我就要开始忙了。
t_en: Is there anything else you want to know? If not, I'll get back to my work.

t_ch: 我有些问题想问…
t_en: I still have some more questions...

t_ch: 矿区最近不太平？
t_en: I heard that the mine hasn't been peaceful lately.

t_ch: 是有点乱，上次你在矿场也见到了吧…不过闹归闹，那些流浪者迟早会消停下来的。
t_en: Yes. As you saw last time in the mine, it's been a bit chaotic... but it will end. The vagrants will stop messing around sooner or later.

t_ch: 毕竟到头来，大家还是要吃饭的嘛。要是真把正经矿队都排挤走了，光凭那些流氓地痞根本养不活自己。
t_en: After all, they've got mouths to feed. If they really got rid of all the professional mining teams, those hooligans won't be able to make a living.

t_ch: 他们自己也清楚这点，所以每次闹事都还算有分寸，稍微尝到点甜头就打道回府了。只是没想到这次居然惊动了史瓦罗……
t_en: They are well aware of this, which is why they have never crossed the line. A small reward goes a long way with them. I just didn't expect the news to get all the way to Svarog this time...

t_ch: 听说你招了些流浪者进矿队？
t_en: I heard you recruited some of the vagrants into the mining team.

t_ch: 唉，怎么说呢…大家都不容易，要是这磐岩镇也被裂界侵蚀了，我们也得像他们一样另寻生计，谁又不希望这时有人能帮一把呢？
t_en: Well, how should I put it...? Life isn't easy. If Boulder Town also gets eroded by the Fragmentum, we will have to find another livelihood. Who wouldn't want some help at a time like that?

t_ch: 可是老矿工们不这么想，他们觉得流浪者把矿队弄得乌烟瘴气的…然后那些新来的又觉得矿队待遇太差，没有自由…到头来搞得两头不讨好。
t_en: But the old miners have their own ideas. They think the vagrants have made a mess of the mining team... and the vagrants aren't happy with their payment and feel that they've lost freedom... In the end, neither side is happy.

t_ch: 不过我有自己的行事准则，这点挫折不会改变我的想法，放心吧。
t_en: But I know what I am doing. This minor setback will not change my mind. Don't worry about it.

t_ch: 地底有什么推荐的活动吗？
t_en: Do you have any recommendations for activities in the underground?

t_ch: 比较出名的是那个搏击俱乐部——但我才不会花钱去看那种滑稽表演，太掉价了，恶俗又低级。
t_en: The Fight Club is quite popular. But personally, I wouldn't pay a shield for that kind of cheap show. It's vulgar and low-class.

t_ch: 还有家大饭店，就是歌蒂开的那家，菜品挺不错——前提是歌蒂没有突发奇想，研究出一些难以下咽的诡异菜谱……
t_en: There is a restaurant, the one owned by Gertie. The food there is quite good... so long as Gertie doesn't come up with some weird recipes on a whim...

t_ch: 另外，流浪者那边好像有说唱表演来着？我欣赏不来，也没去过，只是有所耳闻。你要是感兴趣可以去逛逛，就是去的时候得注意安全。
t_en: Also, I heard there will be a rap concert where the vagrants are? I don't know how to appreciate that kind of thing, so I've never been there. Go check it out if you are interested. Just be careful there.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all questions I have.

t_ch: 没什么事，那我就不打扰你了。
t_en: That's about it. I will leave you to your work.

t_ch: 那行，以后要有机会再合作吧。眼下我还得想下排班的事，回头再聊。
t_en: Okay. I'm looking forward to working with you sometime. I have to figure out scheduling, so I'll talk to you later.

t_ch: 又见面了，外来者。怎么说，要谈生意，还是说你找我有事？
t_en: Hello again, outsider. What brings you here, business or something personal?

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 哦，这不有消息说上下层区马上要重新开放了嘛，我最近正忙得团团转呢。
t_en: Well, rumor has it that the Overworld and Underworld are opening up to each other again, so I've been busy with that.

t_ch: 都忙些什么？
t_en: Busy doing what?

t_ch: 嗐，不过是些生意上的事，我正和上层区的企业家们谈条件呢——虽然到目前为止只是在互相写信罢了。
t_en: It's just a business thing. I'm still ironing out the details with some Overworld entrepreneurs — even if we're still only at the letter-writing stage.

t_ch: 你如何评价解除封锁一事？
t_en: What do you think about the lifting of the blockade?

t_ch: 你问我？要我说，那好处肯定大于坏处。
t_en: You asking me? I reckon the good outweighs the bad.

t_ch: 封锁之后，我只能靠矿场的生意过活——这虽然够我吃穿用度、也够给矿民开工资，但这下层区的经济环境说到底还是死水一潭，资金和物料根本流动不起来……
t_en: During the days of the blockade, I could only rely on my mining for work — and despite leaving me with enough money and my miners' wages, this Underworld and its economy are stagnant. There's no trickling down of funds or goods...

t_ch: 但如今封锁解除，那状况可就大不相同了。我手里的资产现在可以用在各种投资上，等雪球越滚越大再坐享红利——这才是生财之道。
t_en: But now the blockade's lifted — well, things are completely different. I can invest my assets into anything I want, and I'll wait for that compound interest to start snowballing — that's how you make money, see.

t_ch: 你都投资了些什么人？
t_en: Who do you invest in?

t_ch: 我想想啊…有家专做地髓设备的私人工厂，好像还有个生产机器人的民营军工厂。
t_en: Let me think... There's a private manufacturer that makes Geomarrow gear, and another military one that produces robots.

t_ch: 哦，对了，我还联系上了一个叫拉雯妮的房地产中介，她手里的项目看着很有前途…但前提是她所说属实。
t_en: Oh yeah, that's right — I also got in touch with a real estate agent called Lavonne. Her project looks promising... if what she's saying is actually true.

t_ch: 总之…我先观望观望再说。
t_en: In short... I'll have to wait and see.

t_ch: 除了这些，还有什么其他变化吗？
t_en: Any other changes around here besides those?

t_ch: 你这是当调查记者还是商业间谍啊？问得这么仔细…不过我信得过你，毕竟你是救了我们矿区的恩人。
t_en: Are you an investigative reporter, or is this commercial espionage!? All these detailed questions... But I know I can trust you — after all, you're the generous soul one who saved our mine.

t_ch: 要我说，除此之外的最大变化——大概就是我终于有地方能花钱了？比如上层区的那家黄金歌剧院，我老早就想去看看了……
t_en: If you ask me, the other biggest change has got to be — that I've finally got somewhere to spend my money. I've always dreamed of going to the Golden Theater up in the Overworld...

t_ch: 造物之柱的工程也由你负责吗？
t_en: Are you responsible for the Pillars of Creation construction project, too?

t_ch: 哦？你可真抬举我。这么重要的工程，当然是大守护者亲自负责。
t_en: Oh? You flatter me. Such an important project is naturally overseen by the Supreme Guardian.

t_ch: 我呢，只要确保来干活的工人们吃饱穿暖，就算是最大的贡献了。
t_en: As for me, I make sure that the workers are all fed and clothed, that is my greatest contribution.

t_ch: 你觉得造物引擎能修好吗？
t_en: Do you think the Engine of Creation can be fixed?

t_ch: 能啊，必须能。
t_en: Of course, it has to be.

t_ch: 大家都已经把力气用到这份上了，还有什么不能完成的工作？
t_en: Everyone has already worked so hard for this, do you think there is anything that they cannot do?

t_ch: 你怎么看待造物引擎？
t_en: What are your thoughts on the Engine of Creation?

t_ch: 高大，宏伟，比地下的任何工程都要目光长远，承载着我们的希望。
t_en: It is large, grand, and requires greater foresight than any underground construction project. It is a vessel of our hope.

t_ch: 之前的事我也听说了， 这是你们和大守护者共同争取来的机会，我们当然要把握住。
t_en: I've heard about what happened before, that you and the Supreme Guardian fought for this opportunity together. Naturally, we want to seize it.

t_ch: 为了让工程进展顺利，我把一部分积蓄捐给了施工队——这是我最正确的一笔投资。
t_en: In order to keep the construction underway, I donated a portion of my savings to the construction team. It's the best investment I've ever made.

t_ch: 好不容易回到地面上，我可不希望一切都泡了汤。
t_en: We worked hard to make it back up to the surface, I don't want it to all be for nothing.

t_ch: 只是路过，有事先走了。
t_en: I'm just passing by. I've got somewhere else to be.

t_ch: 哼，淡季过去了，拳赛的高峰期就要来了…如果票价提高十个点……
t_en: Hmm, the off-season is over and the busy season for boxing is coming up... If the ticket prices go up by <unbreak>10%</unbreak>...

t_ch: 嗯？你谁啊，买票了么？没买就别在这瞎凑热闹…等会儿，看你这身奇异的装扮，莫非——
t_en: Huh? Who are you? Do you have a ticket? If not, please leave... Hang on, judging by your strange outfit, could you be —

t_ch: 哦，等会儿，看你这身奇异的装扮，莫非……
t_en: Wait a minute. The way you're dressed... Are you...?

t_ch: 哈哈，原来是「外来者」！怪我有眼无珠，多有冒犯，请你不要介意。
t_en: Haha! So you're an outsider! I'm being blind here. Sorry if I offended you.

t_ch: 先自我介绍一下，我是史考特，「搏击俱乐部」的老板。我听他们说，你身手特别好…啧，看你这副模样，卖相倒也不错。
t_en: Let me introduce myself. I'm Scott, the owner of the Fight Club. I heard that you are very good at fighting... Hmm, you also look pretty good.

t_ch: 我这儿就缺你这种人才，怎么样，有没有兴趣跟我签约，成为本赛季的常驻拳手？
t_en: I need people like you here. Do you want to join the club and be a resident boxer for this season?

t_ch: 让我再考虑考虑吧。
t_en: I'll mull it over.

t_ch: 还考虑什么？时机不等人。
t_en: What do you say? Time waits for no man.

t_ch: 我才不跟你这种人签约。
t_en: I'm not signing up with you.

t_ch: 你这家伙，真是不识好歹，哼，回头后悔了可别回来求我。
t_en: Hmph, you'll regret it. Don't come asking me to change my mind later.

t_ch: 行吧，你不是来打拳的，那就是来观赛的咯？
t_en: If you're not here to fight, you must be here to watch.

t_ch: 自己排队买票去，我史考特做生意向来公私分明，别以为跟我聊几句混个脸熟了，就能免票入场。
t_en: Go buy a ticket. I'm an honest businessman. Exchanging a few words with me won't get you free admission.

t_ch: 我不是来看拳赛的。
t_en: I'm not here for the match.

t_ch: 看不起谁呢？
t_en: Don't you look down on me.

t_ch: …怎么说？难道是来找我谈生意的？看你这样子，也不像啊。
t_en: ...Then are you here for business? You don't look like a businessman.

t_ch: 说吧，你找我到底有什么事？
t_en: So, what exactly do you want to see me about?

t_ch: 其实我想问…
t_en: I want to ask you...

t_ch: 嗯？你想问什么？商业机密的话免谈！门儿都没有！
t_en: Hmm? What do you want to know? If you want to know my business secrets, then I'll have to tell you to go away.

t_ch: 给你打拳的都是什么人？
t_en: Who are the fighters?

t_ch: 想在我这儿打拳，你身上必须得有「卖点」。只要能给我带来收益，管你是小丑还是怪物，我统统买单。
t_en: Anyone that wants to be a fighter here needs to have some selling points. As long as they can bring me profit, whether they are clowns or monsters, I'll hire them.

t_ch: 我最擅长鉴别拳手，一眼就能看出哪个有真材实料，哪个是混饭吃的废物。
t_en: I'm the best at seeing through people. I can tell at a glance which boxer has real talent and which one is just a loser.

t_ch: 你问这个干嘛，心动了？想露两手？那刚才怎么不答应我？
t_en: Why are you asking? Are you thinking about giving it a try now? You could have said yes to me earlier.

t_ch: 干这行有赚头吗？
t_en: Is there a lot of money in this line of work?

t_ch: 只要客源不断，收入自然有保障。哈，你不会真以为我只是个卖门票的吧？光靠卖票赚的那点钱够干什么？
t_en: As long as we have a constant flow of customers, income is guaranteed. Hah, do you really think that all I do is sell tickets? What could I do with the tiny bit of money I make from tickets?

t_ch: 我的目标是在地底搞个全链路深度推广，细分观众群体，抓住话题风口，形成拳赛视听娱乐的产业闭环……
t_en: My goal is to create an end-to-end in-depth marketing campaign, segment the audience, seize on the trends, and build an audio-visual entertainment industry around boxing in the Underworld...

t_ch: 然后多方位布局，打通地底所有产业，创造属于我的商业帝国……
t_en: And then link it to all the industries in the Underground to create a business empire...

t_ch: 咳，扯远了，全当我在自言自语吧。你就当没听见，行吧？
t_en: Ahem, I've said too much. Just consider this as me thinking out loud....pretend you heard nothing, okay?

t_ch: 听说你虐待拳手？
t_en: I heard you mistreat the boxers.

t_ch: 扯淡！你听谁说的，他敢当着我的面说吗？
t_en: Baloney! Who said that? Tell them to say it to my face.

t_ch: 自己顶不住不会找我解约吗？实在不行就去别的地方找个班上啊，矿队不就喜欢收那种五大三粗的家伙吗？
t_en: They are welcome to end their contracts and find a job somewhere else if they can't take it anymore. The mining team would love one of these big, burly guys.

t_ch: 再说了，究竟是被我虐待了，还是这个拳手自己内心承受力太弱啊？不知道从自己身上找原因，这就是弱者的心态！
t_en: Maybe they're just too fragile. Instead of reflecting on their own decisions, they accuse me of abuse! The typical mentality of the weak!

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all questions I have.

t_ch: 哼，不知好歹的外来人。
t_en: Hmph, ignorant outsiders.

t_ch: 在这地底要想发财，谁不知道得跟我史考特混。
t_en: Everyone knows, they have to follow Scott if they want to make a life for themselves Underground.

t_ch: 哼，我怎么说来着，机会难得，跟着我吃香的喝辣的是迟早的事。
t_en: Hmph, as I said, opportunity doesn't wait. Getting rich is just a matter of time when you are with me.

t_ch: 不过我先说好，你先前错过了一次好机会，你要找我签约当拳手啊，这底薪和分成的比例可没之前那么高了。
t_en: Let me be clear, you already missed one chance to sign up, so if you want to be a boxer now, the basic salary and profit share ratio won't be as high as before.

t_ch: 拳赛就是这样，观众的喜好随时都在变，合同也得跟着调整——嗯？不对，看你个表情，也不像来找我签约的。
t_en: This is how boxing is. The audience's preferences change all the time, therefore the contract must also be adjusted. Hmm? You don't look like you are here to sign with me.

t_ch: 说吧，你找我到底有什么事？
t_en: So, what exactly do you want to see me about?

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 嚯哈哈哈，你听说了吗？上下层区马上就要重新打通，那帮银鬃铁卫也要下来了！
t_en: Bwahaha, did you hear that? They're lifting the blockade between the Overworld and the Underworld, and the Silvermane Guards will be coming down here!

t_ch: 这件事对你有什么好处？
t_en: What's in this for you?

t_ch: …难道其中藏了我不知道的笑点？
t_en: ...Is there a joke in there that I've failed to understand?

t_ch: 哼，这你就不懂了吧！将来铁卫们肯定会长期驻扎在这里。那么问题来了——这下层区本来也没什么娱乐活动，那他们该去哪儿找乐子呢？
t_en: Hmph, you just don't get it! The Silvermane Guards will definitely be stationed here for a long time. So, here's my question: There's nothing to do for fun down in the Underworld, so what are they going to be getting up to for entertainment?

t_ch: 难道是搏击俱乐部？
t_en: Are you hinting at the Fight Club?

t_ch: 难道是「地火」的集会所？
t_en: Are you hinting at the Wildfire gathering place?

t_ch: 哎——肤浅，真是肤浅！铁卫老爷们可看不上那种地方！
t_en: Oh — that's shallow, reeeal shallow! The Silvermane Guards don't give a dang about that kinda place!

t_ch: 我听说史瓦罗那里有说唱大赛…
t_en: I heard there's a rap battle over at Svarog's...

t_ch: 哎——肤浅，真是肤浅！铁卫老爷们可看不上那种地方！
t_en: Oh — that's shallow, reeeal shallow! The Silvermane Guards don't give a dang about that kinda place!

t_ch: 嚯哈哈哈…告诉你：答案就是我史考特的搏击俱乐部！
t_en: Bwahaha, let me tell you: The answer is my Fight Club!

t_ch: 驻扎在地下的铁卫可不像前线的兄弟们每天出生入死…整天除了巡逻就是站岗，肯定早就技痒难耐了。
t_en: The guards stationed down in the Underworld aren't like those heroes staring down death every day on the frontlines... All they do is stand or patrol. They must be itching to show off their skills.

t_ch: 你也想明白了吧？这种想要上战场的欲望无处发泄，那就只能来我的搏击俱乐部找刺激咯。
t_en: So you get it, too? All this bloodlust with nowhere to vent. All you can do is rock up to the Fight Club and have a good time.

t_ch: 哼哼，我可千万不能放过这种机会——这可是发大财的好时机啊。搞不好还能忽悠几个新兵蛋子来给我打拳，甚至还能在地上开分馆，啧啧啧……
t_en: Hmph, I mustn't waste this opportunity — now's the time when fortunes are made. Maybe I can fool a few rookies to take a crack at me, or even open up a branch up in the Overworld, tsk tsk tsk...

t_ch: 你知道吗，科鲁泽去世了。
t_en: Did you hear? Kluzer passed away.

t_ch: 科鲁泽？科鲁泽…哦，你是说那个，「怪物盔甲」！
t_en: Kluzer? Kluzer... Oh, you mean that thing — "Monster Armor"!

t_ch: 原来他已经入土了啊…我说怎么好几天没见着这人呢。
t_en: So he's kicked the bucket, huh... I was wondering why I hadn't seen him for a few days.

t_ch: 还好，这两天没他比赛，要不他这一出准给我生意搅黄咯。我还得赶紧把后边的安排调整调整……
t_en: Luckily he doesn't have any fights for a while, otherwise he'd definitely mess up my business. I gotta switch things up quickly behind the scenes...

t_ch: 你真是个冷血的混蛋！
t_en: You really are cold-blooded!

t_ch: 冷血？你说我？怎么着，难道你想说是我把他逼上绝路的不成？
t_en: Cold-blooded? Me? What do you mean, are you saying that I forced him down that dead end road?

t_ch: 这地下的大环境就是这样——富贵险中求！你要是想挣大钱，要么自己把命搭进去，要么让别人给你卖命，懂吗？
t_en: That's how things are down in the Underworld — it's high risk, high reward! If you wanna make it big, you have to either stake your life on it, or stake someone else's life on it. Get it?

t_ch: 我就知道你是这反应…
t_en: I knew you'd overreact like that...

t_ch: 哈，看来你也是个识时务的{M#好小子}{F#好姑娘}。
t_en: Ha, looks like you're a {M#guy}{F#gal} who keeps themselves up on all the plays.

t_ch: 这地下的大环境就是这样——富贵险中求啊！你要是想挣大钱，要么自己把命搭进去，要么让别人给你卖命……
t_en: That's how things are down in the Underworld — it's high risk, high reward! If you wanna make it big, you have to either stake your life on it, or stake someone else's life on it...

t_ch: 行了行了，我还有事，可没空一直跟你聊这种无聊的话题……
t_en: Alright, alright. I've got something I need to do, and I don't have the time to keep blathering with you about all this...

t_ch: 啊，{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}，我真的不能收你的礼物啦！
t_en: Huh? Oh, I can't take your gift!

t_ch: 不过，如果一定、一定要送给我的话，人家也可以勉为其难收下的……
t_en: But if you really, really want... I guess I can take it... but I don't wanna...

t_ch: …什么？
t_en: ...Huh?

t_ch: 咦，原来{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}没有准备礼物啊……
t_en: Oh, so you didn't bring a gift...

t_ch: 那…{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}来找阿丽娜有什么事情吗？我可是很忙的哟，请不要占用人家太多时间～
t_en: So... what are you here for? I'm really busy. Please don't take too much of my time.

t_ch: 我只是在闲逛…
t_en: I'm just taking a stroll...

t_ch: 嗯？原来是这样吗？
t_en: Hmm? I see...

t_ch: 倒是也没什么特别的事…
t_en: Nothing really...

t_ch: 唔，原来是这样吗……
t_en: Hmm? I see...

t_ch: 也没关系啦，直觉告诉我，{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}一定是个很可爱的人…阿丽娜想要和可爱的人交朋友！
t_en: It's okay. Alina thinks you are sooo cute... I like being friends with cute people!

t_ch: 那么，以后就请{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}多关照啦！
t_en: It's really nice to meet you!

t_ch: 来聊聊吧…
t_en: Let's get to know each other...

t_ch: 你在等别人送你礼物？
t_en: Are you expecting a gift?

t_ch: {M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}不知道吗？诊所里的其他小朋友每天都会给我送小礼物呢～
t_en: You didn't know? The kids in the clinic give me gifts every day.

t_ch: 为什么呀？
t_en: Why?

t_ch: 这个嘛…我也不知道。可能因为大家都很善良吧～
t_en: Well... I don't know. Maybe they are just nice.

t_ch: 他们会送什么礼物呢？
t_en: What kind of gifts do they give you?

t_ch: 种类还挺多的呢，嘿嘿～不过我最喜欢的就是毛绒玩具啦。
t_en: All kinds of things. Hehe... my favorite is the plush toys.

t_ch: 收到礼物确实会很开心，不过要是数量太多的话，我也会觉得有点不好意思呢…但我也不方便拒绝大家的好意，就只好都收下啦。
t_en: I'm happy to get gifts but sometimes there are too many, and I feel pressured... It's not nice to say no to gifts, so I accept them all.

t_ch: 偷偷告诉你哦，有些时候，还会有小朋友为了送人家礼物吵架，甚至打起来…
t_en: You know what? Sometimes kids even fight over giving me gifts...

t_ch: 我只希望大家能够和睦相处…呜呜。
t_en: I just hope everyone can get along...

t_ch: 送你礼物的孩子…有什么目的？
t_en: Why do they give you gifts... What are they up to?

t_ch: 咿！{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}居然问我这种问题…真是坏心眼！
t_en: Hey! What are you asking me that for?... Don't be a jerk!

t_ch: 这怎么就坏心眼了…
t_en: Why am I a jerk all of a sudden...?

t_ch: 因为确实是这样嘛…有很多小朋友都会送阿丽娜礼物，然后对阿丽娜说「喜欢」……
t_en: They just want to... A lot of kids want to give me things and tell me they like me...

t_ch: 用大人的话说，这就叫「追求」吧？其实我也不太懂啦……
t_en: Grown-ups call that "pursuing someone," but I don't really understand...

t_ch: 总觉得…
t_en: I feel...

t_ch: 这小姑娘话里有话啊…
t_en: She is referring to something else...

t_ch: 咦，{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}说什么啦？如果遇到了什么问题，可以直接对我说哦～
t_en: Sorry, what did you say? You can tell me if you have any problems.

t_ch: 阿丽娜一定很讨人喜欢吧？
t_en: People must like you a lot, Alina.

t_ch: 才没有啦，嘿嘿……
t_en: Not really... Hehe...

t_ch: 不过，我很高兴听到{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}这么说！谢谢你～
t_en: You just made my day! Thank you!

t_ch: 但是，其实诊所里还是有些孩子不愿意和阿丽娜一起玩的……
t_en: Actually, some kids in the clinic don't want to play with me...

t_ch: 为什么？
t_en: Why?

t_ch: 原因的话，我也不是特别清楚啦。
t_en: I'm not sure why.

t_ch: 只是，在诊所里，有很多小朋友的爸爸妈妈都不知道去了哪里，而阿丽娜的爸爸妈妈偶尔还会来看人家……
t_en: A lot of kids in the clinic don't have moms and dads, but my mom and dad come to see me sometimes...

t_ch: 爸爸妈妈来接我的时候，总会看到那些小朋友远远躲着，好像不想让别人看到自己……
t_en: When my mom and dad are here to pick me up, those kids always hide far away. They don't want other people to see them...

t_ch: 我在想…也许，这会是大家不愿意和阿丽娜一起玩的原因吗？
t_en: I think... maybe that's why they don't want to play with me.

t_ch: 就到这里吧。
t_en: I've got to go.

t_ch: 再见啦。
t_en: See you.

t_ch: {M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}再见～！
t_en: Bye bye!

t_ch: {M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}，我们又见面啦！
t_en: It's you again!

t_ch: {M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}今天是特意来看阿丽娜的吗？好开心喔～
t_en: Are you here to see me? I'm so happy!

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 咦？是问我最近有收到什么样的礼物吗？最近的话…有一大包「雪地跳跳糖」，还有一只很可爱的毛绒兔哦！
t_en: Mmm? Are you asking me if I've gotten any nice gifts recently? Well... Recently, there's been a big bag of snow-white popping candy, and also a cute fluffy bunny rabbit!

t_ch: 啊，对了，我还听爸爸妈妈说，我们很快又能去上层区玩了…好期待呀～
t_en: Oh yeah, I also heard from my parents that we'll be able to go to the Overworld soon... I can't wait~

t_ch: 祝你玩得开心。
t_en: Hope you enjoy yourself!

t_ch: 谢谢你，{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}。
t_en: Thank you, {F#ma'am}{M#sir}.

t_ch: 阿丽娜喜欢上层区吗？
t_en: Alina, you must love the Overworld, right?

t_ch: 当然喜欢呀。上层区的孩子们都穿很好看的衣服，还能收到很多精致的小礼物。
t_en: Course I do! The kids in the Overworld all wear beautiful clothes, and they all get pretty little gifts.

t_ch: 听爸爸妈妈说，他们以前就住在上层区，直到家里「破产」了以后才搬到了下层区。如果有机会的话，阿丽娜真想回到上层生活呢。
t_en: My parents told me that they used to live up there until they were "bankrupted" and we had to move down here. If I get the chance, I'm definitely gonna try and go back up there to the Overworld.

t_ch: 下次去上层区玩的时候…我想问问鼹鼠党的小朋友要不要一起去。毕竟一个人玩总会无聊的～
t_en: When I go to the Overworld... I'm gonna ask the Mole kids if they wanna come with me. 'Cos it's boring just playing by yourself.

t_ch: …尤利安就不用问啦，反正他一定会跟我一起去的，嘻嘻。
t_en: ...I don't need to ask Julian. He'll definitely come with me, heehee.

t_ch: 呼噜…呼噜……
t_en: *snore*

t_ch: 该醒醒了！
t_en: It's time to wake up!

t_ch: 呼噜…嗯？谁啊？
t_en: *snore* ...Hmm? Who is it?

t_ch: 哦、哦哦！又见面了，咱们还真有缘。
t_en: Oh... Ooh! We meet again! It seems we have a connection.

t_ch: 你不用上班吗？
t_en: Don't you have to work?

t_ch: 上班…点到即止就行了。
t_en: Work is just a small part of my life.

t_ch: 做一天，然后躺三天，这就是我的工作准则。
t_en: My philosophy is to work for one day and relax for three.

t_ch: 咕噜……
t_en: *rumble*...

t_ch: 哎呀…让你见笑了，嘿嘿，睡了一天，我也该起来吃早饭了。
t_en: Hehe, time to get up for breakfast. I've been sleeping all day.

t_ch: 让你见笑了，嘿嘿，睡了一天，我也该起来吃早饭了。
t_en: Hehe, time to get up for breakfast. I've been in bed all day.

t_ch: 早饭？
t_en: Breakfast?

t_ch: 对啊，我每天一般只吃一顿饭，这第一顿不就是叫「早饭」吗？
t_en: Mm-hmm. I usually just have one meal per day. Isn't the first meal of the day called "breakfast"?

t_ch: 你一天都没吃饭？
t_en: You haven't eaten all day?

t_ch: 才一天，不是很正常吗？没什么好大惊小怪的……
t_en: It's only been one day, which is pretty normal. It's really no big deal...

t_ch: 我的最高记录是连续三天不吃东西，这算什么。
t_en: My record is fasting for three days, so this is nothing.

t_ch: 又不是活不下去，你看，我现在不还活蹦乱跳的吗？嘿嘿…呃，倒也不必用这种怜悯的眼神看着我，没那么夸张吧？
t_en: It's not like I might die. Look, I'm alive and doing just fine. Hehe... And don't look at me with all that pity. It's nothing.

t_ch: 话说回来…你把我叫醒，是有什么事吗?
t_en: By the way... Why did you wake me up again?

t_ch: 其实我很好奇…
t_en: I'm curious...

t_ch: 这么点钱怎么生活？
t_en: How can you live on such a small amount of money?

t_ch: 我开销不多，一人吃饱全家不饿，嘿嘿。你要是学我一天只吃一顿饭，肯定也能省下不少伙食费。
t_en: My expenses are low. One person eats and the whole family is fed. Hehe. If you have one meal a day like me, you could also save a lot on food.

t_ch: 别误会，我可不是为了省钱才只吃一顿的…主要还是因为我每天都会睡到自然醒，不怎么干活，能量消耗自然就少了。
t_en: Don't get me wrong. My goal isn't to save money... It's just that I sleep most of the day and I don't do much work, so my energy consumption is naturally low.

t_ch: 我这人没什么购买欲，凡事只要够用就行——你看我这身衣服裤子，都是旧的！至于其它那些身外之物…仔细想想就能明白，我根本不需要那些玩意！
t_en: I don't have much desires for shopping. I'm happy with what I have. The clothes I'm wearing are old. As for other kinds of worldly possessions... I don't need any of them!

t_ch: 为什么你这么懒？
t_en: Why are you so lazy?

t_ch: …懒？才不是！这只是一种生活方式而已！我又不是什么活都不干，最低限度的工作我还是会按时按量完成的……
t_en: ... Lazy? No, I'm not lazy! It's just my way of life! It's not like I don't do anything at all. I simply do the minimum amount, but I do my work on time...

t_ch: 要我说，挖矿这事根本不值得耗费太多心神和体力。每天就这么低头锄大地，人的思想都会被束缚……
t_en: If you ask me, mining is not worth the effort and energy. Digging every day can trap the mind...

t_ch: 而我挣脱束缚的方式就是——睡觉！通过这种低消耗的行为来审视自己的人生，远离外界的纷扰……
t_en: My way of breaking the cycle is to... sleep! I stay clear of the world's disturbances through this low-cost act and I regularly reflect on my life...

t_ch: 你不怕惹来非议吗？
t_en: Aren't you worried about what people say?

t_ch: 什么非议？我跟大家伙处的都可好了。
t_en: People aren't saying anything. I get along well with everyone.

t_ch: 大家都知道我的脾气，时间长了也都习惯了我的生活方式…说到底，我又没碍着谁的事，也没挡着谁升官发财，对不？
t_en: They all know how I am and over time they have become used to my lifestyle... After all, I'm not in anyone's way, am I?

t_ch: 再说了，这年头谁还顾得上关心别人的生活啊…能管好自己就不错了。
t_en: Besides, no one has the time to obsess about other people's business nowadays... People are lucky just to be able to take care of themselves.

t_ch: 你老了以后怎么办？
t_en: What will you do when you're old?

t_ch: 那么遥远的事，想它干嘛…放心吧，我肯定饿不死的啦！
t_en: That's far in the future. Why think about it now...? Don't worry so much. I won't starve to death!

t_ch: 你看那些卖力气工作的矿工，一天到晚汗流浃背——图个啥？年轻的时候享受不到生活，老了又落下一身病痛……
t_en: Look at those hard-working miners. They are soaked in sweat all day long, and what do they get? They miss out on life when they're young and when they're old, they are sick and in pain...

t_ch: 与其担心我，你不如去关心关心他们。依我看，他们才是可怜人呐……
t_en: You should focus on them instead of worrying about me. In my opinion, they are the poor ones...

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 没什么，不打扰你了。
t_en: Nothing. I will leave you alone.

t_ch: 要我说，人就该丢掉那些不切实际的欲望，回归自然，回归地底……
t_en: If you ask me, humans should just abandon those unrealistic desires and return to nature... return to the underground...

t_ch: 好了好了，我要继续睡了…呼…呼噜……
t_en: Okay, I'm going back to sleep... *snore*...

t_ch: 呼噜…呼噜……
t_en: *snore*

t_ch: 呼——哎哟！我刚做了个美梦…哎，怎么又是你啊？
t_en: *Snore*... Ah! I was having a really nice dream... Huh, it's you again?

t_ch: 等会儿，我睡觉流口水了吗？你怎么用这种眼神看着我…还是说你有什么事想问我？
t_en: Wait a minute. Why are you looking at me like that...? Was I drooling in my sleep or do you have something to ask me?

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 最近？感觉也没什么特殊的变化……
t_en: Nothing special recently...

t_ch: 一定要说的话，我的睡眠质量似乎又上升到了新的高度……
t_en: If you must know, my sleep quality seems to have improved again...

t_ch: 哇哦，真是可喜可贺。
t_en: Wow, that's great.

t_ch: 嗯…但要说为什么会这样，我也没什么头绪…你说，会不会是因为最近温度变低了？
t_en: Yep... But I have no idea why... Do you think it's because the temperature got colder lately?

t_ch: 这是怎么回事呢？
t_en: Why?

t_ch: 这是怎么回事呢？我也没什么头绪…你说，会不会是因为最近温度变低了？
t_en: Why? I have no idea... Do you think it's because the temperature has got colder lately?

t_ch: 大概是吧。
t_en: I guess so.

t_ch: 大概不是吧。
t_en: I don't think so.

t_ch: 这地下的温度居然还变过？
t_en: The temperature can change here?

t_ch: 是啊，其实每天都有细微的变化。一般人可能感觉不到，但这和我的睡眠质量息息相关。
t_en: There are actually subtle changes every day. Others might not notice it, but the changes can affect the quality of my sleep.

t_ch: 不管怎么说，最近的暖气管道确实是不如以前热了。不过稍微凉快点对我来说倒是件好事……
t_en: Anyway, the heating pipes are not as hot as they used to be. But I feel better with the cooler temperature...

t_ch: 桑博都和你聊什么商业机密了？
t_en: What business secrets did Sampo say to you?

t_ch: 也没啥，就是聊了聊我这矿具能租多少钱。
t_en: Nothing really. We were just talking about how much I could get if I rented my mining tools out.

t_ch: 我还以为是什么大事呢。
t_en: I thought you talked about something important.

t_ch: 对吧，确实不是啥大事，但是件好事。
t_en: It isn't important, but it's good.

t_ch: 你就这么把机密交代了？
t_en: You're revealing your secret like that?

t_ch: 这有啥呀，桑博老哥也没和我说不能交代。
t_en: What's the big deal? Sampo didn't say to keep it a secret.

t_ch: 难得碰上这么件好事，我还想和你分享分享呢。
t_en: Such a good thing is hard to come by, and I really want to share it with you.

t_ch: 为什么是件好事？
t_en: Why is it good?

t_ch: 噢，其实这也是我半梦半醒时才意识到的。
t_en: Well, I actually thought this up when I was half asleep.

t_ch: 你想啊，我平时做一休三——一天挣够活三天的钱，后三天不挣钱。
t_en: Think about this — I usually work one day and rest for the next three days, since I can earn enough in a day to support my expenses for the next three days.

t_ch: 但现在可不一样了。只要有人租我的矿具，那么我不出工的三天也能挣到钱！这也就意味着……
t_en: But now it's different. If someone rents my mining tools, I can earn money during the time I don't work! It means...

t_ch: 意味着你能多挣点钱！
t_en: It means you can make more money!

t_ch: 是，但重点并不只是能多挣点钱……
t_en: Yes, but making more money isn't the main point...

t_ch: 意味着你可以不出工了！
t_en: It means you don't have to work anymore!

t_ch: 哟，你这脑子和桑博老哥一样快啊。
t_en: Hah, your mind's just as quick as Sampo's.

t_ch: 总之，我这一合计——如果不用亲自劳动的话，生活开销又能再砍一半…要是我能光靠出租矿具挣钱，那还何必专门跑去矿场工作呢？
t_en: Anyway, based off my calculations, if I don't go to work myself, I can cut my daily expenses in half too... If all I need to do is to rent out my tools, why would I even need to work at the mine?

t_ch: 四舍五入一下，这不相当于让别人替我工作，而且我还有钱挣吗？啧啧，听着就美滋滋啊……
t_en: All in all, wouldn't that mean someone else is doing the work for me, but I'm still earning money? Tsk tsk, the mere thought of it makes me happy...

t_ch: 祝你成功。
t_en: Here's to your success.

t_ch: 哈，借你吉言。
t_en: Ha, I'm counting on it.

t_ch: 呵欠…我这困意好像又有点上来了。今儿就聊到这儿吧，我先休息了……
t_en: *yawn*...I'm getting sleepy again. We'll stop here today. I want to take a nap...

t_ch: 怎么听着有点像工头呢…
t_en: That sounded exactly like a supervisor...

t_ch: 噢…好像是这么个道理。
t_en: Ooh... You're right.

t_ch: 呵欠…问题好像变复杂了，弄得我又有点困。今儿就聊到这儿吧，我先休息了……
t_en: *yawn*... Looks like the problem's become complicated, and it's gotten me all sleepy. Let's talk another time. I'm taking a nap...

t_ch: …这种苔藓，只有极少量会伴生在地髓矿上，它会汲取地髓的精华，因此拥有着强盛的生命力……
t_en: ... Rare moss only growing in the Geomarrow mines! This moss draws on the essence of Geomarrow, and has great vitality...

t_ch: 只要轻轻点燃它，烟雾就会创造一个平和的空间，清除矿场机械在运转时产生的有害磁场，抚平你的身心气息。
t_en: Burn it and its smoke will create a calmness, remove the harmful magnetic fields generated by the operation of the mine machinery, and soothe your mind.

t_ch: 小小一捆，就能净化你周边的环境，帮你提高记忆力与专注力——
t_en: A small bundle is enough to purify your surroundings, improve your memory, and help you focus —

t_ch: 走过路过，不要错过，数量有限的珍稀…哎、哎哟，哪个不长眼的来烦我？
t_en: Don't miss out on this rare item... Hey! How dare you distract me!?

t_ch: 呃…怎么是你？
t_en: Uh... It's you?

t_ch: 你在卖什么？
t_en: What are you selling?

t_ch: 没什么…一点矿坑里的「土特产」而已……
t_en: Nothing... It's just a little specialty from the mine...

t_ch: …你又在骗人！
t_en: ... Are you tricking people again!?

t_ch: 没、真没有！我哪里敢！
t_en: No, I am not! I wouldn't dare!

t_ch: 干嘛用那种眼神看着我？我、我又没做什么坏事。
t_en: Why are you looking at me like that? I—I didn't do anything wrong.

t_ch: 我怎么没听说过这苔藓？
t_en: I've never heard of this type of moss before.

t_ch: 这是磐岩镇的特产，你们小年轻的见识少很正常……
t_en: It's a specialty of Boulder Town. Younger people usually don't know much about it...

t_ch: 难道你已经改邪归正了?
t_en: So you're on the right path now?

t_ch: 那当然，我现在可是已经浪子回头了！
t_en: Of course. I'm a completely different person now.

t_ch: 你也听到了吧？我可是在一开始就介绍得很清楚了……
t_en: You heard my sales pitch, right? I made everything very clear...

t_ch: 我不过是再卖些无害的苔藓，我、我可没有招摇撞骗！
t_en: I'm just selling some harmless moss. I'm not tricking anyone!

t_ch: 你刚才明明在喊…
t_en: You were shouting out that...

t_ch: 「伴生在地髓矿上」？
t_en: It grows in Geomarrow mines?

t_ch: 是长在矿坑里啊。这附近只有地髓矿洞，长在同一片土壤上，「伴生」不就是「一起生长的同伴」的意思……
t_en: That's right. The Geomarrow mine is the only mine in these lands, and the moss also grows around here. So if they are on the same soil...

t_ch: 我这个说法也没问题吧？
t_en: I'm being honest, no?

t_ch: 「能清除有害磁场」？
t_en: What about "it can remove harmful magnetic fields"?

t_ch: 熏香嘛，至少能净化下空气……
t_en: Well, it's incense, so it can at least purify the air...

t_ch: …至于磁场，虽然眼睛看不见，但你也不能断定它不存在，是吧？
t_en: As for the magnetic field, although its not visible, you can't fully deny its existence, right?

t_ch: 「提高记忆力与专注力」？
t_en: What about "improve memory and help focus"?

t_ch: 呃、呃，苔藓晒干以后用法和熏香差不多，就点燃了烧一下，闻闻味道——
t_en: Well... um... dried moss is similar to incense. You burn it and enjoy the scent...

t_ch: 盯着烟雾放空一段时间，休息够了，这人的记忆力和专注力自然就上去了。非常合理，非常科学，我可没糊弄人啊！
t_en: Let your mind relax for a while, get enough rest, and your memory and focus will naturally improve. It's logical and scientific. I'm not fooling anyone!

t_ch: 你那套说辞全是糊弄人的。
t_en: Your story is all a sham.

t_ch: 别狡辩了，你和娜塔莎说去吧。
t_en: Save it for Natasha.

t_ch: 哎？别啊，我这都很坦诚了呀？我真没有骗人！
t_en: What? Please, I'm being honest. I'm not lying!

t_ch: 都是一点心理安慰，苔藓本身也是无毒无公害我辛苦挖了后晒干的！
t_en: It was just for the placebo effect. The moss itself is non-toxic and harmless. It took me a long time to dig it up and dry it!

t_ch: 求你放过我吧，可千万别和诊所的那个女魔头说啊…她、她太可怕了！我再也不敢了！
t_en: Please let me go. Don't tell her... S—She's terrifying! I won't do it again! I promise!

t_ch: 数量有限，先到先得，老顾客重复购买有九折优惠！
t_en: Limited quantity, first-come, first-served. <unbreak>10%</unbreak> discount for regular customers!

t_ch: 地髓矿的伴生苔藓，清除有害磁场、驱逐不利……
t_en: Moss from the Geomarrow mine. It can remove harmful magnetic fields and unhealthy stuff from the air...

t_ch: 睡前熏一熏，家里诸事顺利，睡得安心，熏得放心！
t_en: Use before bed — then a good sleep and a peaceful mind are guaranteed!

t_ch: 哪个没长眼的又来——哎哟，怎么又是你？我、我可没骗人啊，只是正好路过，我这就走！
t_en: What the heck... you again? I'm not selling anything. I just happened to be passing by. See you!

t_ch: 呣…「雪地跳跳糖」又吃完了……
t_en: Hmm... I'm out of snow-white popping candy...

t_ch: 嗨，尤利安！
t_en: Hi, Julian!

t_ch: 嗨，陌生人！
t_en: Hi!

t_ch: 你在做什么呀？
t_en: What are you doing?

t_ch: 没什么，我只是……
…唔，你是谁？妈妈说过，我不能和陌生人说话。
t_en: Wait, who are you? Mom told me not to talk to strangers.

t_ch: 我们之前一起玩过捉迷藏…
t_en: We played hide-and-seek together before...

t_ch: 什么捉迷藏…？完全没印象。
t_en: Hide-and-seek...? That doesn't ring a bell.

t_ch: 你这不是已经说了嘛…
t_en: You are the one talking to me...

t_ch: …还不是{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}先来搭话的嘛。
t_en: ...You came up to me first.

t_ch: 我说…{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}不会是认错人了吧？
t_en: Hey, did you mistake me for someone else?

t_ch: 难道真的是我认错了？
t_en: Did I?

t_ch: 当然啦，我才不是什么「千面尤利安」呢。
t_en: Of course. I'm not "Julian of a Thousand Faces."
